reasoning: As of December 21, 2025, the World Health Organization has not declared any disease a pandemic in 2025. A thorough search of recent WHO press releases and global news shows that while there was much discussion about the 2025 Pandemic Agreement and the 5-year anniversary of the COVID-19 pandemic, there are no new pandemic declarations. Furthermore, the WHO actually wound down the mpox Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) around September 2025. Given that only 10 days remain in the year and there is no fast-moving global outbreak currently escalating to PHEIC status—a necessary precursor to any pandemic declaration—the chance of a new pandemic being declared before the end of 2025 is vanishingly small. The Polymarket price of 1% on December 11 already reflected this near-impossibility, and the passage of 10 more days makes it even less likely. Therefore, the probability is set at the minimum allowed bound of 2%.
